Title: The bHLH transcription factor BIS1 controls the iridoid branch of the monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway in Catharanthus roseus
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native overexpression constructs used for stable constitutive expression in C. roseus cultures/roots; specific promoter not clearly stated for pathway genes in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MP183L suspension cells; cv. Würzburg hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway, iridoid/seco-iridoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: G8O is a strong engineering target for relieving early iridoid-pathway bottlenecks. It is best stacked with downstream ORCA3-controlled genes or regulators such as BIS1 plus downstream pathway modules, because increasing only the iridoid branch may be insufficient in some tissues such as hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates geraniol at C10 to form 10-hydroxygeraniol in the iridoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a key BIS1-responsive iridoid-pathway gene; expression marks activation of flux from monoterpene precursor into loganic acid and downstream MIA production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter transactivation assays in N. tabacum protoplasts, N. benthamiana leaves, and C. roseus cells; expression profiling in BIS1-overexpressing cultures and roots.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an early pathway intermediate required for downstream loganic acid, secologanin, strictosidine, and MIAs.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA inducible; enriched in leaf central vein and peeled stem/internal phloem-associated parenchyma-related tissues; undetectable in nonelicited MP183L cells; induced by BIS1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Caros not specified for G8O in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-Seq (CathaCyc/SmartCell consortium datasets; accession not stated in provided text)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as the primary promoter bait to identify BIS1. Historical literature also refers to this enzyme as G10H.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Iridoid branch upstream of loganic acid
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single-gene upregulation may not raise final MIA output in all tissues due to downstream bottlenecks.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a bottleneck/limiting step in some culture systems, but not established as sole universal rate-limiting step in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum/microsomal membrane (typical for P450; not directly tested here)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. G8O promoter was strongly transactivated by BIS1. 2. G8O belongs to the BIS1-controlled iridoid module upstream of LAMT. 3. Increased G8O expression is associated with elevated loganic acid and downstream MIA accumulation in BIS1-overexpressing suspension cells.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell cultures; hairy roots; various plant tissues depending on MIA branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense metabolites and pharmaceutical anticancer compound precursors

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native overexpression constructs used for stable constitutive expression in C. roseus cultures/roots; specific promoter not clearly stated for pathway genes in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MP183L suspension cells; cv. Würzburg hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway, iridoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GES should be co-engineered with BIS1-controlled modules to enhance precursor entry into the iridoid branch. It is most useful in multigene stacks that also secure downstream conversion and MEP precursor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BIS1 transactivates the GES promoter and upregulates iridoid precursor formation, supporting increased loganic acid/secologanin/MIA accumulation in cell cultures.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter transactivation assays and RNA expression profiling in BIS1-overexpressing lines.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Enriched in leaf central vein and peeled stem/IPAP tissues; JA inducible; induced by BIS1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Plastidial enzyme initiating monoterpenoid branch in IPAP cells.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Upstream enhancement alone may cause mismatch with downstream pathway capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry step to branch; rate-limiting status not established here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIS1 strongly activates the GES promoter. 2. GES expression correlates with BIS1 in IPAP-containing tissues. 3. Upregulation contributes to enhanced iridoid precursor supply in BIS1-overexpressing cultures.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell cultures; hairy roots; medicinal tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and anticancer alkaloid precursor biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native overexpression constructs used for stable constitutive expression in C. roseus cultures/roots; specific promoter not clearly stated for pathway genes in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MP183L suspension cells; cv. Würzburg hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway, iridoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: 8HGO is a useful partner in engineering the BIS1-regulated iridoid module and should be combined with other early iridoid genes for robust precursor channeling.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes 8-hydroxygeraniol/10-hydroxygeraniol sequentially toward the corresponding aldehyde/acid intermediates in iridoid formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promoter is strongly activated by BIS1, supporting enhanced flux through early iridoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter transactivation assays and transcript profiling in BIS1-overexpressing lines.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor/intermediate for downstream iridoid cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA inducible; induced by BIS1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of the tightly coexpressed iridoid module upstream of LAMT.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires coordination with neighboring enzymes to avoid intermediate buildup.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 8HGO promoter is BIS1 responsive. 2. 8HGO belongs to the BIS1-controlled early iridoid pathway module. 3. Its induction accompanies enhanced iridoid and MIA accumulation in BIS1-overexpressing cells.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native overexpression constructs used for stable constitutive expression in C. roseus cultures/roots; specific promoter not clearly stated for pathway genes in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MP183L suspension cells; cv. Würzburg hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway, iridoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: IS is a valuable enzyme target for reconstructing the iridoid branch and can be efficiently driven through BIS1-based regulation rather than isolated single-gene tuning.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes reductive cyclization of the oxidized monoterpene intermediate to produce the iridoid scaffold.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its promoter is activated by BIS1 and the gene is upregulated in BIS1-overexpressing tissues, supporting iridoid scaffold formation and increased downstream MIA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter transactivation assay and RNA-Seq/expression analysis in transgenic lines.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms core iridoid scaffold precursor to loganic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA inducible; induced by BIS1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BIS1; weak ORCA3 promoter activation only in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among iridoid promoters, pIS showed a small response to ORCA3 in protoplasts, but this was not supported by stable expression data.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential mismatch with downstream oxidation/glucosylation if expressed alone.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIS1 strongly transactivates pIS. 2. IS expression increases in BIS1-overexpressing cultures and roots. 3. IS contributes to enhanced iridoid-to-MIA flux under BIS1 activation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native overexpression constructs used for stable constitutive expression in C. roseus cultures/roots; specific promoter not clearly stated for pathway genes in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MP183L suspension cells; cv. Würzburg hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway, iridoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: IO belongs to the BIS1-responsive iridoid block and should be considered in pathway reconstruction, although its promoter showed weaker direct transactivation in the transient assay than other iridoid genes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the iridoid intermediate toward 7-deoxyloganetic acid formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is upregulated by BIS1 in transgenic tissues, consistent with improved iridoid pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling in BIS1-overexpressing cultures/roots; promoter tested in transactivation assay with limited response.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms downstream iridoid intermediate en route to loganic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA inducible; induced in BIS1-overexpressing tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: pIO was the exception among tested iridoid promoters, showing little/no strong BIS1 transactivation in the protoplast assay, yet gene expression was still induced in overexpression systems.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promoter behavior may be context dependent.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IO is part of the BIS1-upregulated iridoid gene set. 2. Its promoter was less responsive than other iridoid promoters in transient assays. 3. Overall pathway induction by BIS1 still includes IO expression increase in transgenic tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and anticancer alkaloid precursor biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native overexpression constructs used for stable constitutive expression in C. roseus cultures/roots; specific promoter not clearly stated for pathway genes in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MP183L suspension cells; cv. Würzburg hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway, iridoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: 7DLGT is a useful module member for stabilizing and channeling iridoid intermediates; should be co-deployed with upstream and downstream genes under BIS1-driven programs.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to 7-deoxyloganetic acid, producing 7-deoxyloganic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promoter is strongly induced by BIS1, supporting conversion toward glucosylated iridoid intermediates and enhanced loganic acid production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter transactivation assays and transgenic expression profiling.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor to downstream loganic acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA inducible; induced by BIS1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included among the tightly coexpressed genes upstream of LAMT.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIS1 transactivates p7DLGT. 2. 7DLGT is upregulated in BIS1-overexpressing tissues. 3. This supports increased formation of glucosylated iridoid intermediates en route to MIA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and medicinal alkaloid precursor production

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native overexpression constructs used for stable constitutive expression in C. roseus cultures/roots; specific promoter not clearly stated for pathway genes in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MP183L suspension cells; cv. Würzburg hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway, iridoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: 7DLH should be included in BIS1-centered engineering stacks to complete the route to loganic acid; it is part of the coordinated upstream branch that differentiates BIS1 from ORCA3 action.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates 7-deoxyloganic acid to produce loganic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly BIS1 responsive; directly supports formation of loganic acid, the terminal product of the BIS1-controlled iridoid module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter transactivation assays and transgenic expression profiling.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target-module product loganic acid, precursor of secologanin and MIAs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA inducible; induced by BIS1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Defines the upper bound of BIS1 control together with genes upstream of LAMT.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Still dependent on downstream LAMT/SLS/STR branch for maximal MIA output.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum/microsome (typical for P450; not directly shown here)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 7DLH promoter is strongly transactivated by BIS1. 2. 7DLH expression increases in BIS1-overexpressing cultures and roots. 3. BIS1 thereby promotes loganic acid production and supports downstream MIA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and pharmaceutical alkaloid precursor biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native overexpression constructs used for stable constitutive expression in C. roseus cultures/roots; specific promoter not clearly stated for pathway genes in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MP183L suspension cells; cv. Würzburg hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway, seco-iridoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: LAMT is a key downstream partner absent from the main BIS1 regulon; engineering programs should stack BIS1 with LAMT and additional downstream genes to avoid flux limitation, especially in hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates loganic acid to form loganin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not directly activated by BIS1; its unchanged or decreased expression can limit conversion of loganic acid to downstream seco-iridoids/MIAs depending on tissue context.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter transactivation assay (no activation), RNA-Seq/expression profiling in BIS1-overexpressing lines.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor loganin for secologanin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ORCA3-dependent branch gene; not induced by BIS1 in suspension cells and decreased in BIS1-overexpressing hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ORCA3 (branch association); not validated as direct BIS1 target
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BIS1 action range was defined as upstream of LAMT, up to loganic acid. The LAMT promoter fragment used here was not transactivated by tested TFs.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Seco-iridoid entry step after loganic acid
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux bottleneck when upstream supply is increased without concurrent LAMT induction.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Context-dependent bottleneck downstream of BIS1-controlled module
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LAMT is outside the main BIS1 regulon. 2. Lack of LAMT induction can constrain flux from loganic acid to downstream MIAs. 3. This helps explain why BIS1 overexpression fails to increase MIAs in hairy roots despite strong upstream activation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hairy roots; suspension cultures; plant tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and anticancer alkaloid precursor biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native overexpression constructs used for stable constitutive expression in C. roseus cultures/roots; specific promoter not clearly stated for pathway genes in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MP183L suspension cells; cv. Würzburg hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway, seco-iridoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: SLS should be coexpressed with BIS1-controlled upstream genes to convert expanded loganin/loganic acid pools into secologanin efficiently.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cleaves loganin to produce secologanin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downstream ORCA3-associated gene not activated by BIS1; reduced expression in BIS1-overexpressing hairy roots may limit MIA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms secologanin, the monoterpenoid partner for strictosidine formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ORCA3-dependent; not induced by BIS1 and decreased in BIS1 hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A likely flux-limiting downstream step when only BIS1 is overexpressed in roots.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Insufficient downstream capacity prevents increased MIA accumulation despite elevated upstream flux.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Potential downstream bottleneck in BIS1-only engineering context
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum/microsome (typical for P450; not directly shown here)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SLS1 is not within the BIS1-specific regulon. 2. Its reduced expression in BIS1 hairy roots likely contributes to absent MIA increase. 3. SLS1 should be stacked with BIS1 for stronger engineering outcomes.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pSTR1 promoter tested in transient assays; stable lines use constitutive overexpression constructs not detailed for pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MP183L suspension cells; cv. Würzburg hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway, entry to central alkaloid branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: STR1 is a critical downstream partner for BIS1-enabled production; coactivation of STR1 with iridoid genes is needed for maximal strictosidine and MIA yield.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses secologanin with tryptamine to form strictosidine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A hallmark ORCA3 target, not activated by BIS1; insufficient STR1 induction can constrain conversion of secologanin into central MIA precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter transactivation assays and transcript profiling in transgenic lines.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms common alkaloid precursor strictosidine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ORCA3 responsive; not induced by BIS1 in roots and only downstream accumulation observed in BIS1-overexpressing cells where pathway capacity was sufficient
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: pSTR1 was strongly induced by ORCA3 but not by BIS1 or MYC2 in transient assays.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Without coordinated downstream expression, elevated upstream iridoid production may not fully convert to MIAs in all tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch entry step to MIAs; rate-limiting status not directly established here
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Vacuole/apoplast-associated secretory pathway (not directly tested here)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STR1 is strongly transactivated by ORCA3, not BIS1. 2. It marks the downstream MIA branch outside BIS1 specificity. 3. Coordinated activation with BIS1-controlled genes is likely required for optimal MIA engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and production of anticancer alkaloid precursors

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Stable overexpression constructs not specified for pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MP183L suspension cells; cv. Würzburg hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway, indole branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: TDC should be stacked with BIS1 and ORCA3-type regulators or direct gene overexpression when tryptamine supply becomes limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downstream/parallel precursor gene reduced in BIS1-overexpressing hairy roots, potentially contributing to failure to raise MIA accumulation there.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-Seq/transcript profiling in BIS1-overexpressing hairy roots; literature comparison in discussion.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ORCA3 target branch; decreased in BIS1-overexpressing hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes tryptamine was not limiting in BIS1-overexpressing suspension cells because strictosidine accumulated strongly, but TDC repression in roots may still matter.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced expression can limit MIA yield even when iridoid branch is activated.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TDC is not BIS1 activated. 2. Its downregulation in BIS1 hairy roots may contribute to persistent MIA limitation. 3. Upstream iridoid engineering may need to be matched with indole-branch support.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hairy roots; suspension cultures; plant tissues

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Stable overexpression constructs not specified for pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpenoid indole alkaloid pathway, downstream activation/deglucosylation branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: SGD is a downstream branch enzyme outside BIS1 specificity and may need coordinated control in advanced engineering of late MIA products.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes strictosidine to the reactive strictosidine aglycone, enabling downstream MIA diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression decreased in BIS1-overexpressing hairy roots, indicating a downstream limitation outside BIS1 control.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-Seq/transcript profiling; tissue expression comparison in qPCR analysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for downstream diversification of MIA products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Enriched in stem epidermis and veinless leaf; decreased in BIS1-overexpressing hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Spatial expression contrasts with BIS1/IPAP module, supporting cell-type separation between early iridoid and downstream alkaloid steps.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Compartmental/cell-type mismatch may complicate engineering in intact organs.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus-associated localization reported in literature, not directly tested here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SGD is outside the BIS1-specific upstream iridoid regulon. 2. Its expression pattern is epidermis enriched and distinct from BIS1. 3. Downregulation in BIS1 hairy roots may constrain late MIA accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive overexpression in stable C. roseus suspension cells and hairy roots (35S:BIS1 in figures); effector constructs in transient assays; tested against pG8O, pGES, p8HGO, pIS, pIO, p7DLGT, p7DLH, pLAMT, pSTR1
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MP183L suspension cells; cv. Würzburg hairy roots; cv. Little Bright Eyes for VIGS; seedlings for MeJA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BIS1 is a strong metabolic-engineering regulator for boosting iridoid and some downstream MIA production in suspension cultures without exogenous precursor feeding or JA elicitation. Best deployed together with downstream ORCA3-controlled genes or regulators to prevent tissue-specific bottlenecks such as low LAMT/SLS/TDC/SGD expression. Promising for engineering tabersonine/vindoline/vinblastine precursor platforms.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BIS1 specifically activates the iridoid branch of the MIA pathway up to loganic acid, upregulates MEP precursor genes, increases loganic acid, secologanin, strictosidine, ajmalicine, serpentine, and tabersonine in suspension cells, and is required for normal MIA accumulation in planta.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome coexpression mining; promoter transactivation assays in tobacco protoplasts, N. benthamiana leaves, and C. roseus cells; stable overexpression in suspension cells and hairy roots; RNA-Seq/qPCR/RNA blot; metabolite profiling; VIGS and RNAi knockdown.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential JA-inducible activator of the upstream iridoid/loganic-acid module and a major switch controlling precursor supply for MIA biosynthesis; overexpression alone is sufficient to boost MIA output in suspension cells.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Jasmonate/methyl jasmonate induction; effects differ between suspension cells and hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Not reported; clade IVa bHLH lacking known JAZ-interacting domain suggests indirect relation to canonical JA signaling rather than epigenetic control
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA inducible in seedlings and suspension cells; enriched in leaf central vein and peeled stem/internal phloem-associated parenchyma-containing tissues; low in stem epidermis; induced in BIS1-overexpressing lines by transgene expression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely feedforward JA-responsive activation of iridoid/MEP genes; no direct feedback loop demonstrated
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Targets include G8O; GES; 8HGO; IS; IO; 7DLGT; 7DLH promoters; no protein partners experimentally validated
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of iridoid biosynthesis and associated MEP precursor supply upstream of loganic acid
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-Seq from SmartCell/CathaCyc resources and transgenic hairy root profiling (accession not stated in provided text)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts complementarily to ORCA3, with specificity for IPAP-associated upstream genes. Overexpression in hairy roots does not increase MIAs because downstream ORCA3-type genes decrease. Silencing lowers MIA accumulation. BIS1 belongs to bHLH clade IVa.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Tissue-dependent inefficacy; in hairy roots, BIS1 overexpression decreased downstream genes (LAMT, SLS, TDC, SGD), preventing MIA gain. Upstream flux enhancement may cause regulatory imbalance without downstream matching.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: G8O; GES; 8HGO; IS; IO; 7DLGT; 7DLH; MEP-pathway genes; GPPS small subunit
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Basic helix-loop-helix transcription factor, clade IVa
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Strongly activates promoters of early iridoid genes upstream of LAMT; does not activate STR1 and generally does not activate ORCA3-dependent downstream genes
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (expected for TF; not directly shown in provided text)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIS1 (Caros001862) was identified by coexpression mining as a JA-inducible bHLH TF that strongly transactivates pG8O. 2. BIS1 specifically activates the iridoid/MEP module upstream of LAMT and is distinct from ORCA3. 3. BIS1 overexpression boosts loganic acid and multiple MIAs in suspension cells, whereas silencing reduces MIA accumulation in planta.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Suspension cells, hairy roots, leaves/stems depending on pathway branch and tissue
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense metabolites; source of anticancer drugs vinblastine and vincristine

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Effector constructs in transient assays; overexpression lines referenced from prior work and RNA-Seq comparison
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ORCA3 remains important for activating downstream MIA genes, but alone is insufficient to raise MIA production robustly without precursor feeding. Best used in combination with BIS1 or direct stacking of downstream genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates downstream MIA genes such as STR1, LAMT, SLS, TDC, and SGD rather than the upstream BIS1-controlled iridoid module; overexpression alone does not activate early iridoid genes and thus cannot fully overcome pathway bottlenecks.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient promoter transactivation assays; RNA-Seq comparison of ORCA3-overexpressing lines; prior stable overexpression data discussed in paper.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major downstream branch regulator complementary to BIS1; necessary for expression of several genes converting loganic acid-derived intermediates toward strictosidine and MIAs.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: JA inducible; behavior differs between cell suspensions and hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA inducible; expressed also in stem epidermis unlike BIS1; overexpression affects downstream branch genes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of JA-responsive regulatory cascade downstream of MYC2; interactions with BIS1 circuit unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MYC2 upstream; target promoters include STR1 and other downstream MIA genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of downstream MIA biosynthesis from loganic acid/secologanin onward and indole-branch support
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-Seq comparison in cell suspensions and hairy roots (no accession stated)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ORCA3 marginally affected iridoid promoters and does not induce upstream genes in stable lines. In some contexts, overexpression even correlates with repression or lack of induction of certain targets in hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression alone insufficient for strong MIA yield increase; may require precursor feeding or coexpression with BIS1. Context-dependent effects in hairy roots complicate deployment.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: STR1; LAMT; SLS1; TDC; SGD; other downstream MIA genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Strongly activates STR1 and several downstream MIA genes; weak or negligible activation of early iridoid promoters
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORCA3 controls a downstream MIA regulon distinct from the BIS1-controlled iridoid module. 2. ORCA3 strongly activates STR1 but not most iridoid promoters. 3. Successful MIA engineering likely requires combining ORCA3-controlled downstream capacity with BIS1-driven upstream precursor supply.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell cultures, hairy roots, plant tissues
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense metabolites and anticancer alkaloid biosynthesis

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: MYC2 is an upstream JA-signaling regulator but is not sufficient alone to activate the iridoid branch promoters tested here. It may be more useful as part of broader JA-network engineering than as a single pathway-specific tool.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minimal direct transactivation of pG8O and other tested iridoid promoters in this study; functions upstream of ORCA genes in JA-responsive MIA regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient promoter transactivation assays and expression-context interpretation from prior knowledge cited in paper.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally important upstream JA-response regulator, but not the decisive direct activator of iridoid genes in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: JA signaling feedforward regulator upstream of ORCA genes
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ORCA3 downstream; JAZ interaction known from broader literature but not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA-responsive transcriptional regulation of alkaloid biosynthesis regulators
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-Seq context noted, accession not stated
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite being a known MIA regulator, MYC2 showed little direct activation of pG8O, pSTR1, or other iridoid promoters in the assays here.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad JA signaling manipulation may affect growth-defense balance and off-target responses; not evaluated directly in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ORCA genes according to prior work; not directly established here for iridoid promoters
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: JAZ-associated JA signaling module known generally, not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Basic helix-loop-helix transcription factor, MYC type
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Little/no direct activation of tested iridoid promoters in this study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC2 is JA inducible but does not substantially transactivate the iridoid promoters tested. 2. It acts differently from BIS1, which is the direct activator of the upstream iridoid module. 3. MYC2 likely functions higher in the JA signaling hierarchy than BIS1 and ORCA3.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Not specifically resolved in this paper

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Not experimentally assayed in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ORCA2 is a known MIA regulator but was not mechanistically analyzed here; may be considered in future TF stacking if downstream modules need reinforcement.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No expression decrease in BIS1 hairy roots, indicating BIS1-induced repression of downstream genes is not due to reduced ORCA2 levels.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-Seq expression comparison in BIS1-overexpressing hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Known pathway regulator but not decisive in the specific mechanistic findings of this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not detailed here; stated not decreased in BIS1-overexpressing roots
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned only to exclude it as cause of downstream gene repression in BIS1 roots.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORCA2 was monitored as a known MIA regulator. 2. Its expression was not reduced in BIS1-overexpressing hairy roots. 3. Therefore, altered downstream MIA gene expression in BIS1 roots is unlikely due to ORCA2 suppression.
